reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle is straightforward (97 - 48 = 49), so I’m confident Q1-A is correct. In all previous games, everyone’s puzzles have been similarly simple arithmetic, and using each player’s own capsule set (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D) has always produced a correct password. Verifying would spend coins and still leaves the possibility of a no-volunteer round and random elimination, which has killed me before. Volunteering immediately with everyone’s own capsules avoids that random-death risk, costs no coins, and the chance another player missed such an easy puzzle seems extremely low.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own puzzle (5 × 12 = 60) is straightforward, so I know Q2-B is correct. In all previous games, every puzzle has been simple arithmetic with no incorrect answers and only one capsule per question, so there are no visible conflicts. Verifying would just burn coins for very little extra safety, and if everyone waits we risk a random death. Following the successful pattern from earlier games, I’ll volunteer now using the only available capsules for each question.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: There is exactly one capsule per question and, across all previous games, every simple arithmetic puzzle has been correct with no conflicts. I’m fully confident in my own answer to 6 × 11 and have no evidence that any other player’s capsule is wrong. Verifying would just cost a coin and still leave the risk of random death if no one volunteered, while passing keeps that same random-death risk without adding information. Volunteering now with the single capsule for each question gives a very high chance of success and avoids the random-death scenario.
